output:
# HAZE and PRISM: Treating the Quantum-Kernel Encoding Bandwidth as a Per-Feature Relevance Field instead of a Single Global Isotropic Constant in Supervised Quantum Machine Learning

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Supervised quantum machine learning (QML) has converged on a dominant production recipe: take classical data x, map it into a quantum state with a fixed quantum feature map U(x), and compute a fidelity quantum kernel k(x, x') = |<0|U†(x)U(x')|0>|^2, then hand that Gram matrix to a classical support-vector machine (SVM). This is the engine of the quantum-enhanced feature-space line of Havlicek et al. [43], the quantum-SVM line of Rebentrost-Mohseni-Lloyd [46], the quantum-embedding line of Lloyd et al. [47], and hardware demonstrations on superconducting [48] and nuclear-spin [49] processors. The reason the kernel viewpoint is central, and not just one option among many, is Schuld's structural result [37]: essentially ALL supervised QML models that encode data and read out a linear observable ARE kernel methods, so the feature map IS the model. Jager and Krems [50] sharpened this by showing variational quantum classifiers and quantum-kernel SVMs share the same expressivity class, and data re-uploading [44] is a re-encoding device for enriching that same feature space. In short: in supervised QML, choosing the encoding is choosing the learner.

Within this recipe, one hyperparameter has emerged as the single most decisive control on whether the kernel generalizes at all: the encoding BANDWIDTH (data scaling). Before encoding, each feature x_j is multiplied by a scale beta, so the rotation angle that carries feature j is proportional to beta * x_j. Without bandwidth control, quantum kernels suffer EXPONENTIAL CONCENTRATION (Thanasilp, Wang, Cerezo, Holmes [C1]): as the qubit count n grows, off-diagonal kernel values k(x, x') for x != x' concentrate exponentially around a fixed constant, the Gram matrix collapses toward the identity, the SVM can only memorize, and resolving the kernel at all requires exponentially many measurement shots. Shaydulin and Wild [C2] identified that a single scalar bandwidth beta is the lever that controls this, and Canatar, Peters, Pehlevan, Wild, and Shaydulin [C3] proved that tuning that scalar is what RESTORES generalization in quantum kernel models. Bandwidth tuning is therefore the current state of the art: a globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel is the strongest, most defensible baseline the field has.

The silent assumption underneath this state of the art is that bandwidth is a single GLOBAL, ISOTROPIC SCALAR -- one number beta, applied identically to every feature dimension, every qubit, and every data point. This implicitly asserts that all input coordinates deserve equal encoding resolution and that the data manifold is isotropic in feature space. But real classical datasets are violently anisotropic: features differ by orders of magnitude in label-relevance, scale, noise, and redundancy, and most coordinates are nearly irrelevant. A single global beta is then forced into an impossible compromise -- large enough to resolve the few informative features means over-resolving the many irrelevant ones (each irrelevant coordinate's rotation pushes states apart, manufacturing orthogonality and ACCELERATING concentration without adding label signal), while small enough to suppress the irrelevant coordinates means under-resolving the informative ones (the kernel collapses toward all-ones and loses the very structure it needs). This is precisely the regime in which the field's most prominent NEGATIVE results live: Slattery et al. [42] found "numerical evidence against advantage with quantum fidelity kernels on classical data," and Kubler, Buchholz, and Scholkopf [C4] showed that a quantum kernel's inductive bias is set by the encoding and is frequently MISALIGNED with the target on classical data. The pipeline already computes every signal needed to diagnose this per-feature -- the Gram matrix, single-feature kernels, label correlations -- yet it allocates encoding resolution with one global knob and measures nothing about which features deserve it.

This is the opening this work targets: a measured-but-not-allocated gap between a rich set of per-feature relevance signals on one side and a single global isotropic bandwidth on the other. Positioned against prior art, the bandwidth line [C2, C3] introduced exactly ONE global scalar; the trainable-embedding line [47, C5] retrains the WHOLE encoding by expensive gradient descent (barren-plateau-prone) rather than allocating a calibrated per-feature field; the classical automatic-relevance-determination (ARD) line [C6] has per-feature length-scales but only for classical kernels with no notion of quantum concentration; and the negative-result line [42, C4] documents the symptom without naming the allocation knob that causes it. Nothing promotes bandwidth from a scalar to a calibrated, free-to-estimate per-feature field, and that disconnect is what we name and close.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

We name the gap HAZE (Homogeneous-bandwidth Assumption Zeroing per-feature Encoding-relevance adaptation). The standard recipe defines an encoding rule that applies one bandwidth beta to every feature, implicitly asserting that the right encoding resolution is a GLOBAL CONSTANT, the same beta_j == beta for all coordinates j. HAZE states that the right encoding bandwidth is in truth a HETEROGENEOUS per-feature FIELD beta: {1..d} -> R+, where beta_j is the resolution feature j deserves given its label-relevance, its contribution to concentration, and its redundancy with other features. Formally, the deployed objective trains an SVM on the global-bandwidth Gram k_beta with beta_j == beta_0 for all j; this is merely the ISOTROPIC special case of a per-feature-bandwidth kernel, and whenever the realized relevance field is non-uniform, the isotropic choice either over-resolves noise directions (driving concentration, per [C1]) or under-resolves signal directions (collapsing the kernel) -- and on an anisotropic dataset it must do at least one of these somewhere.

The defining property of HAZE is that the harm is SILENT under pooled test accuracy and CONCENTRATES in a structured subpopulation. Pooled accuracy is an average over a test set, so on a dataset where most features are irrelevant the global bandwidth's misallocation manifests not as a uniform drop but as a collapse of separability that bites hardest in the HIGH-DIMENSION / LOW-RELEVANT-FRACTION regime -- exactly the regime where the negative results [42] congregate and where quantum kernels were hoped to help. And the harm is a QUANTUM-SPECIFIC MULTIPLIER: every irrelevant feature encoded at full bandwidth contributes to exponential concentration [C1], so the damage from over-resolving noise compounds with qubit count in a way that has NO classical analog (a classical RBF kernel does not concentrate exponentially from extra irrelevant length-scales the way a fidelity kernel does). The harm VANISHES if and only if the relevance field is uniform, beta_j == beta_0 for all j: when every feature genuinely deserves identical resolution (a low-dimensional, all-relevant, isotropic dataset), the global bandwidth is already optimal and HAZE disappears. The gap exists exactly because real data is anisotropic.

We introduce PRISM (Per-feature Relevance-Informed Spectral Modulation), which promotes the bandwidth scalar to a field and acts on it with two orthogonal knobs. The metaphor is exact: PRISM takes the single "white" global bandwidth and REFRACTS it into a per-feature spectrum, giving each feature the encoding resolution it earns. The field is estimated FOR FREE (or at low, bounded overhead) from signals the pipeline already produces, at a deliberately DE-CONCENTRATED reference bandwidth where kernel values are shot-resolvable: (e1) marginal kernel-target alignment -- the centered alignment of the single-feature kernel k^(j) (encoding feature j alone) with the label kernel yy^T, a cheap measure of how much feature j alone separates the classes (the Cristianini alignment / Hubregtsen KTA [C5] applied per coordinate); (e2) concentration contribution -- how much adding feature j to the encoding shifts the off-diagonal Gram statistics toward concentration WITHOUT increasing label-aligned variance, read directly from kernel statistics that must be computed anyway, penalizing features that buy orthogonality but no signal; and (e3) redundancy -- the classical correlation / HSIC overlap of feature j with already-selected features, so resolution is not spent twice on the same direction. The raw estimate rho_hat_j is passed through a small held-out CALIBRATION map c(.) -- fit by isotonic or Platt scaling on a PROBE split where per-feature usefulness IS measured (held-out validation lift of including-feature-j-at-bandwidth vs excluding it) -- yielding rho_j = c(rho_hat_j), anchored so that at the reference relevance c maps to the global value. KNOB1 (selection / gating): g_j = 1[rho_j >= gamma] decides which features are encoded at all (g_j = 0 sets feature j's rotations to identity -- dropped from the feature map, directly removing a concentration source). KNOB2 (reweighting / continuous bandwidth): beta_j = beta_0 * psi(rho_j) allocates per-feature resolution (psi monotone non-decreasing, psi(reference) = 1) -- the quantum analog of ARD [C6], but where the per-feature scale now ALSO controls quantum concentration. The PRISM feature map is U_PRISM(x) = U_beta(x) with the per-feature bandwidth vector beta = (g_1 beta_1, ..., g_d beta_d), and the kernel is k_PRISM(x, x') = |<0|U_PRISM†(x) U_PRISM(x')|0>|^2, fed to the same classical SVM.

The decisive design property is the EXACT-COLLAPSE guarantee [provable-by-construction]. The collapse is a DESIGN SWITCH that disables per-feature adaptation, not a claim that the true field is uniform: set rho_hat_j to the reference value for all j (disable estimation), so rho_j = c(reference) maps to the anchor, hence g_j = 1[anchor >= gamma] = 1 for any gamma <= anchor, beta_j = beta_0 * psi(anchor) = beta_0 * 1 = beta_0, and therefore U_PRISM(x) = U_{beta_0}(x), the global-bandwidth map. Entry-by-entry, k_PRISM(x, x') = k_{beta_0}(x, x') = k_global(x, x'), so PRISM reproduces the globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel Gram matrix exactly, with per-entry discrepancy E_collapse = max_{x, x'} | k_PRISM(x, x') - k_global(x, x') | <= 1e-6 enforced by a CI-gateable build check (E_collapse <= 1e-6 is a finite-precision implementation budget, not a performance claim). This is a strict, safe generalization: zero regression at the identity default, which here is the CURRENT STATE OF THE ART (global bandwidth tuning [C2, C3]), not a weak baseline.

The novelty wedge is four clauses that, taken together, no prior line covers more than two of: C1 -- the encoding bandwidth is modeled as a per-feature FIELD beta_j, not a global isotropic constant (scalar -> field promotion); C2 -- the field is estimated for free / low-overhead from signals the kernel pipeline already produces (marginal alignment + concentration contribution + redundancy) at a de-concentrated reference, with NO d-dimensional bandwidth grid search and NO barren-plateau-prone variational retraining of the encoding, passed through a small held-out calibration map c(.); C3 -- TWO orthogonal knobs act on the SAME calibrated field, a selection gate (gamma) and a continuous bandwidth reweighting (psi); C4 -- an EXACT-COLLAPSE strict generalization with a CI-gateable E_collapse <= 1e-6 back to the globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel (zero regression at the SOTA identity default). The quantum-specific twist that distinguishes C2/C3 from classical ARD is that the per-feature field does DOUBLE DUTY: it sets inductive bias (as classical ARD does) AND controls exponential kernel concentration (which has no classical analog [C1]).

BOUNDARY RECOVERIES (named baseline => exact special point of the PRISM field) [provable-by-construction]:

  R1 Trivial all-ones kernel: beta_0 -> 0 or gamma > max_j rho_j (gate all features out) => U == I => k == 1 (rank-1 Gram) => SVM returns the majority/constant predictor. The maximal-concentration limit.
  R2 Global-bandwidth tuned quantum kernel (Shaydulin-Wild [C2] / Canatar et al. [C3]): rho_j == reference (uniform) => g_j = 1, beta_j = beta_0 for all j => k_PRISM == k_global => SVM is identical. This is the deployed STATE OF THE ART and the collapse target.
  R3 Raw, un-tuned feature map (Havlicek-style ZZ map [43], no bandwidth): beta_0 = 1, psi == 1, g == 1 => the over-expressive, exponentially concentrating kernel [C1]. Recovered at beta_0 = 1.
  R4 Pure feature-selection (KNOB2 off, psi == 1): a hard relevance gate choosing which features enter the encoding, all at one global beta_0 -- a "quantum hard-ARD" selection baseline.
  R5 Pure continuous reweight (KNOB1 off, gamma = 0 => g_j = 1 for all j): the continuous per-feature quantum-ARD bandwidth field with no selection.
  R6 Oracle per-feature bandwidth (ceiling): beta_j tuned against TRUE held-out validation lift (or a labeled relevance oracle) => upper-bound ceiling of the field.

Load-bearing symbols: x classical input in R^d; x_j feature j; U_beta(x) bandwidth-beta quantum feature map; |phi_beta(x)> = U_beta(x)|0> embedded state; k_beta(x, x') = |<phi_beta(x)|phi_beta(x')>|^2 fidelity kernel; beta_0 the single global bandwidth (status quo); beta_j per-feature bandwidth field; rho_hat_j free per-feature relevance estimate; c(.) calibration map; rho_j = c(rho_hat_j) calibrated relevance; gamma gate threshold; g_j selection indicator; psi continuous reweight map; k_global the global-bandwidth Gram (R2); k_PRISM the PRISM Gram; E_collapse exact-collapse budget; epsilon do-no-harm margin; keep_rate fraction of features kept; reference = the relevance value at which c anchors to beta_0 (identity default = disable estimation, rho_hat_j = reference for all j).

## 3. Novel Research Idea (5-Question Formulation)

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Does promoting the quantum-kernel encoding bandwidth from a single global isotropic scalar to a per-feature relevance field beta_j -- estimated for free from marginal kernel-target alignment, per-feature concentration contribution, and redundancy, calibrated on a small probe, and used to both gate and continuously rescale each feature's encoding -- improve generalization and recover quantum-kernel separability on anisotropic classical data, without regressing at the identity default, relative to a globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

P1 (the status quo it disrupts: one global isotropic bandwidth). The dominant recipe for supervised QML builds a fidelity quantum kernel from a fixed feature map and trains a classical SVM, and because all such models ARE kernel methods [37], the encoding is the learner. The field has correctly identified that a single scalar bandwidth is what rescues quantum kernels from exponential concentration and enables generalization [C1, C2, C3] -- this is the genuine state of the art. PRISM disrupts the unexamined ISOTROPY in that cure: HAZE asserts that bandwidth is a heterogeneous per-feature field, and the deployed global-bandwidth kernel is merely its uniform special case. The community found the right knob and then froze it to one number; PRISM lets the knob vary across features.

P2 (the silent, quantum-specific harm: misallocated resolution that manufactures concentration). On anisotropic data, one global bandwidth must over-resolve the many irrelevant features or under-resolve the few relevant ones. Over-resolving is uniquely damaging in the quantum setting: each irrelevant coordinate encoded at full bandwidth pushes states apart and ACCELERATES exponential concentration [C1], so the Gram matrix drifts toward identity, the SVM memorizes, and shot cost to resolve the kernel explodes -- a failure mode with no classical analog. The harm CONCENTRATES in the high-dimensional, low-relevant-fraction regime -- exactly where "no advantage on classical data" was reported [42] and where inductive-bias mismatch bites [C4] -- and is INVISIBLE in pooled accuracy. Every detection signal (single-feature kernels, Gram statistics, label correlations) is already computed, yet none is fed back into per-feature resolution allocation: the measured-but-not-allocated gap.

P3 (why it advances knowledge: an unmeasured isotropy assumption becomes a calibrated, inspectable field). The contribution is epistemic before it is empirical. PRISM turns the unmeasured assumption (bandwidth is isotropic) into a calibrated, inspectable per-feature quantity rho_j = c(rho_hat_j), estimated from e1 marginal alignment, e2 concentration contribution, and e3 redundancy, then anchored by a small probe. This reframes the community's question from "is THIS feature map good?" (a tournament among ZZ maps, projected kernels, re-uploading circuits) and "what is the best global bandwidth?" into "WHICH features deserve encoding resolution, and how much?" -- a per-coordinate, stratifiable question. It yields a reusable RELEVANCE-PROBE protocol (AUROC / ECE of rho against an oracle relevance label) by which any feature map can be audited, independent of the dataset it is run on. Naming the field and measuring it is the durable knowledge; the two knobs are the first thing one does once the field exists.

P4 (practical payoff: cheap, encoding-agnostic, drop-in, risk-free adoption). The estimator signals are cheap: single-feature kernels are shallow few-qubit circuits estimated at a de-concentrated reference bandwidth (bounded shot cost), Gram statistics for e2 are a byproduct of the kernel you must compute anyway, and e3 is purely classical -- so PRISM avoids both the d-dimensional bandwidth grid AND the barren-plateau-prone variational retraining of a deep encoding [47]. PRISM is therefore encoding-agnostic (ZZ / Pauli feature maps [43], angle encoding, projected quantum kernels [C7], data re-uploading [44]) and head-agnostic (classical SVM or VQC [50]), and drops into an existing pipeline as a per-feature gate g_j and bandwidth beta_j. Adoption is risk-free by construction: disabling estimation (set rho_hat_j = reference for all j) reproduces the globally bandwidth-tuned kernel entry-for-entry with E_collapse <= 1e-6, removing the usual barrier to touching a working pipeline; a do-no-harm non-inferiority margin epsilon underwrites the near-identity regime.

P5 (the stakes: this is the dominant axis of the quantum-advantage debate). The central open question of supervised QML on classical data is whether quantum kernels can EVER beat classical ones, with strong negative evidence [42] and inductive-bias skepticism [C4] on one side and constructed separations [C8] and bandwidth-enabled generalization [C3] on the other. PRISM targets the exact mechanism that the negative results implicate: misallocated, isotropic encoding resolution. By measuring and reallocating that resolution, PRISM can RECOVER quantum-classical separability (a larger geometric difference g [C7]) that uniform bandwidth destroys -- engaging the negative result head-on rather than around it. The stakes are concrete: on near-term hardware where every shot and every qubit is scarce, spending encoding resolution only on features that earn it is the difference between a kernel that resolves and one that concentrates, for free, with a provable safety net back to the current SOTA.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

O1 CIRCULARITY [REPRESENTATIONAL]. The relevance field is estimated from kernels, but the kernel PRISM is trying to fix may already be concentrated. NAIVE FAILS: estimating rho_hat_j from the full, global-bandwidth (possibly concentrated) kernel means concentration corrupts every per-feature signal -- a concentrated kernel reports near-uniform alignment for ALL features, so rho_hat is flat and PRISM degenerates to R2 exactly when HAZE is worst. REPRESENTATIONAL because the fix is a structural choice about WHERE the estimate is read. MITIGATION: estimate e1/e2 from SINGLE-FEATURE kernels at a deliberately DE-CONCENTRATED reference bandwidth (small beta, where kernel values are resolvable and shot-bounded), never from the full concentrated map; cross-fit rho on disjoint data folds. Falsified by the PLACEBO rung; bounded by the ORACLE rung.

O2 SHOT NOISE AND RESOLVABILITY [REPRESENTATIONAL]. Quantum kernel entries come from finite measurement shots, and a concentrated kernel needs EXPONENTIALLY many shots to resolve [C1]; per-feature diagnostics are even noisier. NAIVE FAILS: estimating relevance at the deployed bandwidth can demand a prohibitive shot budget, or return noise dressed as signal. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the reference bandwidth into the method -- relevance is defined and read at a resolvable scale, not the deployed one. MITIGATION: fix a de-concentrated reference where the shot cost to separate single-feature kernel values is provably bounded; report the shot budget as a first-class metric; show rho is stable as shots increase.

O3 CALIBRATION WITHOUT PER-FEATURE LABELS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. KNOB1's threshold and KNOB2's bandwidth need rho_j to behave like "the resolution feature j deserves," but there is no ground-truth per-feature bandwidth. NAIVE FAILS: raw alignment scores are uncalibrated and incomparable across features and datasets, so thresholding them conflates "high raw score" with "deserves resolution." REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the calibration map c(.) into the architecture -- rho_hat is never used directly; only rho = c(rho_hat) drives g_j, beta_j. MITIGATION: fit c(.) on a small held-out probe where per-feature usefulness IS measured (validation lift of include-at-bandwidth vs exclude) via isotonic/Platt, anchored at the reference so EXACT-COLLAPSE holds; report ECE(rho) far below ECE(raw rho_hat). The LEAKAGE rung keeps the probe split disjoint and frozen.

O4 CONFOUNDING KEEP-RATE / EFFECTIVE DIMENSION WITH RELEVANCE [REPRESENTATIONAL]. KNOB1 changes how many features (hence qubits / encoding depth) are active, and model capacity alone changes generalization. NAIVE FAILS: a "we selected features and improved" result cannot distinguish gains from MEASURING relevance (the claim) from gains from merely training a smaller, lower-capacity kernel (a confound) -- smaller quantum kernels also concentrate less for free. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces a control INTO the design. MITIGATION: a dimension-matched, rate-matched PLACEBO replaces rho by a RANDOM field at the SAME keep_rate / active-qubit count; report keep_rate and active-feature count with every accuracy number; PASS requires PRISM > placebo at matched keep_rate beyond seed noise.

O5 FEATURE INTERACTIONS AND NON-ADDITIVITY. Entangling gates couple features, so per-feature marginal relevance can misjudge JOINT relevance (a feature useless alone but decisive in an XOR-like pair). NAIVE FAILS: greedy single-feature alignment drops a feature that only matters in conjunction, destroying the signal the quantum kernel was uniquely positioned to capture. MITIGATION: include the redundancy/interaction channel e3 (pairwise HSIC / conditional alignment) and a cheap joint-refinement pass over the gated set; test explicitly on datasets with PLANTED feature interactions (parity / XOR-structured labels) where marginal scores are deliberately misleading.

O6 METRIC BLINDNESS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. Pooled test accuracy averages over the test set; on a dataset where the relevant-fraction is moderate, the misallocation harm (and the PRISM fix) barely moves the mean. NAIVE FAILS: reporting only pooled accuracy HIDES both HAZE and the fix, which concentrate in the high-dimension / low-relevant-fraction tail. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the EVALUATION's structure -- the primary readouts must be stratified, since the method's value is the GRADIENT of lift across relevance-ratio and dimensionality strata. MITIGATION: stratified accuracy as the PRIMARY readout, cells = {dimensionality d} x {relevant-fraction low/med/high} x {training-set size}; pooled accuracy reported only for non-inferiority.

O7 HARDWARE NOISE INDUCES ITS OWN CONCENTRATION. On NISQ devices, device noise alone drives kernel concentration [C9], confounding the per-feature relevance signal with qubit-specific error. NAIVE FAILS: a relevance field fit on a noisy device can encode "which qubits are least noisy" instead of "which features are most relevant." MITIGATION: estimate rho on a noiseless / low-noise simulator or on error-mitigated kernels, then transfer; report rho quality and PRISM lift separately for simulator vs hardware; map features to qubits to disentangle relevance from qubit-noise.

WHY IT IS NONETHELESS TRACTABLE. Every obstacle is matched by a structural feature already in the frame: circularity (O1) and shot-resolvability (O2) are fenced by reading the estimate from single-feature kernels at a de-concentrated reference; the missing-label problem (O3) is solved by a tiny calibrated probe rather than per-feature labels; the capacity confound (O4) and metric blindness (O6) are neutralized by the dimension-matched PLACEBO and the mandated stratified readout; interactions (O5) are caught by the redundancy channel and a joint pass; hardware noise (O7) degrades gracefully because rho is estimated off-device and transferred. Above all, the EXACT-COLLAPSE identity (set rho_hat_j = reference => k_PRISM == k_global, E_collapse <= 1e-6) guarantees PRISM does no harm relative to the deployed global-bandwidth SOTA -- the downside is pinned to zero by construction, so the entire research risk is on the UPSIDE.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

Prior art falls into five families; for each we give representative work, what it does, the precise delta PRISM adds, and the boundary recovery (R2-R6) it maps to.

(1) Quantum feature maps / quantum kernels -- Havlicek et al. [43], Schuld [37], Rebentrost-Mohseni-Lloyd [46], Liu-Arunachalam-Temme [C8], projected quantum kernels [C7]. WHAT: define an encoding U(x) and the fidelity kernel, then train an SVM; the encoding is fixed and bandwidth is global or unitary. DELTA: none allocates a per-feature bandwidth field; they assert isotropic resolution. PRISM adds the full wedge C1 (scalar -> field), C2 (free estimator + calibration), C3 (two knobs), C4 (exact collapse). Used at one global scale these map to R2/R3.

(2) Bandwidth and concentration -- Shaydulin-Wild [C2], Canatar et al. [C3], Thanasilp et al. [C1], Wang et al. [C9]. WHAT: identify that a SINGLE global scalar bandwidth controls exponential concentration and enables generalization. DELTA: this is exactly R2, the collapse target -- one number for all features. PRISM's delta is the scalar -> per-feature field promotion that does double duty (inductive bias AND concentration control), with selection and calibration the scalar line never needed because it never had a field.

(3) Trainable / data-driven embeddings -- Lloyd et al. [47], Hubregtsen et al. [C5], covariant kernels [C10], data re-uploading [44]. WHAT: TRAIN encoding parameters (often by maximizing kernel-target alignment) via gradient descent, or enrich the map by re-encoding. DELTA: these retrain the WHOLE deep encoding -- expensive, barren-plateau-prone [C11], no selection gate, no per-feature CALIBRATED relevance field, no exact-collapse safety net. PRISM allocates a cheap, calibrated per-feature field with two knobs and a provable collapse, NOT a full variational retraining; it maps to R5 only in the limit of continuous reweight with no selection and no training.

(4) Classical automatic relevance determination / feature selection -- ARD kernels [C6], kernel-target alignment [C5 origin], influence/valuation methods. WHAT: per-feature length-scales or selection for CLASSICAL kernels, scoring features by relevance. DELTA: classical ARD has NO notion of exponential kernel concentration -- in the quantum setting the per-feature scale ALSO controls a failure mode [C1] with no classical analog, and the estimator must be read at a de-concentrated reference under shot noise. PRISM is the quantum port where the field does double duty; pure-selection maps to R4, pure-reweight to R5.

(5) Negative results / inductive-bias critiques -- Slattery et al. [42], Kubler et al. [C4]. WHAT: document that quantum fidelity kernels show no advantage on classical data and that their inductive bias is often misaligned. DELTA: they diagnose the SYMPTOM (no advantage, bad bias) without naming the ALLOCATION knob (isotropic bandwidth) that causes it or offering a calibrated per-feature fix. PRISM names HAZE as a mechanism behind these results and tests whether reallocating resolution recovers separability -- turning a negative result into a falsifiable, addressable hypothesis.

THE TWO BARRIERS THAT KEPT IT UNSOLVED. (a) It LOOKED like per-feature bandwidth needed either a d-dimensional hyperparameter grid (exponential in feature count) or full variational training of the encoding (barren plateaus [C11], costly on hardware), both prohibitive. The UNLOCK is that per-feature relevance can be estimated FOR FREE from single-feature alignment + concentration contribution + redundancy at a de-concentrated reference, with only a tiny probe to fit c(.) -- no grid, no deep gradient training. (b) Concentration control and feature relevance were studied in SEPARATE communities: the quantum-bandwidth line produced one global scalar [C2, C3], and the classical-ARD line produced per-feature length-scales [C6] with no concept of quantum concentration. Nobody UNIFIED them into a single calibrated per-feature field that simultaneously sets inductive bias AND tames exponential concentration, with an exact-collapse back to the global-bandwidth SOTA (R2) that neither line needed, because neither had named the field.

ONE CLEAN NOVELTY SENTENCE. PRISM is the first method to model the quantum-kernel encoding bandwidth as a calibrated, free-to-estimate per-feature relevance FIELD that simultaneously controls inductive bias and exponential concentration, acting on it with BOTH a selection gate (gamma) and a continuous bandwidth reweighting (psi), while provably collapsing to the globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel at the identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6).

STRONGEST PRIOR-ART RISK (flagged honestly). The most dangerous neighbor is family (3)/(4): trainable embedding kernels [C5] and classical ARD [C6]. A reviewer could argue that "optimize per-feature encoding scales by alignment" is just ARD-meets-kernel-target-alignment repackaged. PRISM's defensible deltas are three and concrete: (i) QUANTUM DOUBLE DUTY -- the per-feature field controls exponential CONCENTRATION [C1], a failure mode absent in classical ARD, and the estimator is consequently read at a de-concentrated reference under a reported shot budget; (ii) FREE CALIBRATED FIELD + TWO KNOBS -- rho is a probe-calibrated per-feature quantity (reported with AUROC/ECE vs an oracle) driving BOTH a selection gate AND a continuous bandwidth, not a single gradient-trained scale, and avoiding barren plateaus by not training the deep encoding at all; (iii) EXACT COLLAPSE -- a CI-gateable strict generalization back to the global-bandwidth SOTA with E_collapse <= 1e-6, which neither trainable-embedding nor ARD pipelines provide. The novelty is not "tune the encoding" -- it is MEASURING per-feature resolution as a calibrated field that tames a quantum-specific failure mode, and ACTING on it with two unified knobs under a provable safety net.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD PIPELINE (end-to-end). Step 0 Choose encoding: any feature map (ZZ / Pauli [43], angle encoding, projected quantum kernel [C7], or a re-uploading circuit [44]) defines U_beta(x). Step 1 Estimate rho_hat_j FOR FREE at a de-concentrated reference bandwidth from e1 marginal kernel-target alignment of the single-feature kernel k^(j) with yy^T, e2 the per-feature concentration contribution read from off-diagonal Gram statistics (penalizing orthogonality without label-aligned variance), and e3 classical redundancy (correlation / HSIC vs already-selected features), cross-fit on disjoint folds to avoid circularity (O1). Step 2 Calibrate: rho_j = c(rho_hat_j) with c(.) fit on a small held-out probe (validation lift of include-at-bandwidth vs exclude) via isotonic/Platt, anchored at the reference so c(reference) maps to beta_0. Step 3 KNOB1 gate: g_j = 1[rho_j >= gamma] selects which features are encoded (g_j = 0 sets feature-j rotations to identity). Step 4 KNOB2 reweight: beta_j = beta_0 * psi(rho_j) (psi monotone, psi(reference) = 1) sets each kept feature's bandwidth. Step 5 Build k_PRISM from U_PRISM(x) with bandwidth vector (g_1 beta_1, ..., g_d beta_d) and train a classical SVM (or VQC head). Safety rails are folded in: de-concentrated reference and cross-fit teacher (O1/O2), do-no-harm non-inferiority gate (epsilon), and the EXACT-COLLAPSE check (set rho_hat_j = reference => k_PRISM == k_global, E_collapse <= 1e-6).

DATASETS / BENCHMARKS. (a) SYNTHETIC with controlled anisotropy -- planted datasets where labels depend on k-of-d features with a tunable relevant-fraction and tunable feature interactions (linear, and parity/XOR for O5); the "labels generated by a quantum kernel" construction used to exhibit separations [C7]; and the discrete-log / fully-quantum dataset of Liu-Arunachalam-Temme [C8] as a positive control. (b) REAL classical benchmarks standard in QML -- PCA-reduced MNIST and Fashion-MNIST subsets (as in [48], [C7]), UCI tabular sets (e.g., the ad-hoc, plasticc-style, and fraud/credit datasets), and the small scikit-learn datasets used in the negative-result study [42] and in the bandwidth study [C3], to engage "no advantage on classical data" directly. (c) HARDWARE -- a small projected/fidelity-kernel case on a superconducting device (following [48]).

BACKBONES (field is encoding-agnostic): ZZ / Pauli feature maps [43], angle encoding, projected quantum kernels [C7], data re-uploading [44]; classical-SVM head and VQC head [50].

BASELINES = boundary recoveries R1..R6 + a strong competitor. R1 trivial all-ones; R2 globally bandwidth-tuned quantum kernel [C2, C3] -- the named SOTA and COLLAPSE TARGET; R3 raw un-tuned feature map [43]; R4 pure feature-selection gate (KNOB2 off); R5 pure continuous quantum-ARD reweight (KNOB1 off); R6 oracle per-feature bandwidth (ceiling); PLUS a trainable-embedding-kernel competitor [C5] to show PRISM matches or beats gradient-trained encodings at a fraction of the cost and without barren plateaus.

METRICS. Test accuracy / AUROC, POOLED and STRATIFIED by dimensionality x relevant-fraction x training-size (stratified is PRIMARY); kernel concentration ratio (variance of off-diagonal Gram entries, to show PRISM DE-CONCENTRATES vs R3); geometric difference g vs the best classical kernel [C7] (to quantify recovered potential advantage, engaging [42]); calibration of the field, AUROC and ECE of rho vs oracle relevance labels (raw rho_hat vs calibrated, to show the lift); keep_rate and active-feature/qubit count, reported with every accuracy number (dimension-matched placebo); shot budget / measurement cost (to evidence "for free"); and E_collapse (the exact-collapse budget, CI-gateable, required <= 1e-6).

ABLATION GRID. Orthogonal 2x2 {KNOB1 on/off} x {KNOB2 on/off} (recovers R2, R4, R5, and full PRISM at the four corners); estimator channels {e1, e2, e3, and all combos} x {raw rho_hat vs calibrated c(.)}; gamma sweep (endpoints recover R1 gate-all-out and R2 gate-all-in); reference-bandwidth sweep (de-concentrated -> deployed, to expose circularity O1 and shot cost O2); psi reweight sweep (mild -> aggressive); cross-fit vs in-sample relevance teacher (to expose circularity O1).

FALSIFIER-LADDER RUNS (explicit PASS conditions). ORACLE (ceiling, R6): replace rho_hat by true validation-lift relevance -- PASS if R2 < PRISM <= oracle AND the oracle shows real headroom; FALSIFIES (estimator) if free rho_hat is no better than PLACEBO. PLACEBO (dimension-/rate-matched random field): random relevance at the SAME keep_rate / active-qubit count -- PASS if PRISM > placebo beyond seed noise at matched capacity; FALSIFIES if the gain was merely a smaller, less-concentrated kernel. RECOVERY (reproduces SOTA): set rho_hat_j = reference -- PASS if k_PRISM == k_global entry-for-entry, E_collapse <= 1e-6, reproducing R2 to seed; FALSIFIES (construction) on any deviation > 1e-6. LEAKAGE: probe split for c(.) disjoint from test, rho never reads test labels, calibration split frozen -- PASS if gains survive the strict disjoint split; FALSIFIES if gains vanish when leakage is removed. DO-NO-HARM: low-dimensional, all-relevant, isotropic datasets (where global bandwidth is already right) -- PASS if accuracy >= R2 - epsilon on EVERY cell; FALSIFIES on any regression beyond epsilon at the identity/near-identity default.

EXPECTED OUTCOMES (as HYPOTHESES; provable items flagged). H1 [conjectured->measured]: a MODEST pooled accuracy lift over global-bandwidth R2 but a LARGE lift in the high-dimension / low-relevant-fraction strata -- the GRADIENT of lift across relevance-ratio is itself the evidence that HAZE is real. H2 [conjectured->measured]: both knobs contribute -- KNOB1 removes outright-irrelevant features that drive concentration, KNOB2 recovers borderline features a hard gate would waste; full PRISM > R4 and > R5 individually. H3 [conjectured->measured]: PRISM measurably DE-CONCENTRATES the kernel (lower off-diagonal Gram variance, higher resolvable signal at fixed shot budget) vs raw R3 -- directly connecting per-feature bandwidth to the concentration phenomenon [C1]. H4 [conjectured->measured]: PRISM increases the geometric difference g vs the best classical kernel on anisotropic datasets, recovering separability that uniform bandwidth destroys -- engaging the negative result [42] head-on. H5 [conjectured->measured]: e1 (marginal alignment) is the strongest single channel on additive datasets but FAILS on planted-interaction (parity) datasets where e3 + joint refinement is required -- confirming O5 is real, not a strawman. H6 [conjectured->measured]: calibrated rho achieves AUROC well above 0.5 vs oracle relevance with ECE far below raw rho_hat, and free rho approaches the ORACLE (R6) ceiling while clearly beating the dimension-matched PLACEBO; PRISM matches or beats the trainable-embedding competitor [C5] at a fraction of the shot/optimization cost. P1 [provable-by-construction]: EXACT-COLLAPSE -- set rho_hat_j = reference => k_PRISM == k_global, E_collapse <= 1e-6 (zero regression at the SOTA identity). P2 [provable-by-construction]: boundary recoveries R1..R6 are exact special points of the field. P3 [provable-by-construction underwriting a conjectured->measured outcome]: DO-NO-HARM non-inferiority within epsilon at the identity/near-identity default, underwritten by P1; note P1 holds exactly under exact arithmetic, while empirical near-identity behavior under finite shots and approximate calibration is a separate, measured claim.

Responsible and Reproducible-Research Considerations. (1) Honest-advantage framing: PRISM is a method to REPAIR quantum-kernel generalization on classical data, NOT a claim of exponential quantum advantage; any geometric-difference gain (H4) is reported against the strongest available classical kernel and dequantization checks, and all advantage claims are scoped strictly to the tested datasets, encodings, and qubit counts. (2) Concentration honesty: because exponential concentration [C1] can make small-qubit successes fail to scale, we report the kernel concentration ratio and shot budget AS A FUNCTION OF qubit count and never extrapolate generalization claims beyond the simulated/measured n. (3) Placebo and keep-rate governance: keep_rate and active-qubit count are REQUIRED (not optional) reporting items alongside every accuracy number, and the dimension-matched PLACEBO is mandatory so "we selected features" can never masquerade as "we measured relevance." (4) Hardware-vs-simulator separation: simulator and hardware numbers are NEVER pooled; rho quality, concentration ratio, and PRISM lift are reported separately for noiseless simulation, noisy simulation, and real device, with the device, calibration date, and error-mitigation method documented (O7). (5) Probe and leakage governance: the calibration probe for c(.) is disjoint from and frozen relative to the test set, with a minimum probe size reported and the full split released; all H1-H6 are explicitly HYPOTHESES, and the provable EXACT-COLLAPSE identity does NOT imply empirical non-inferiority under finite shots. (6) Reproducibility: encodings, bandwidth references, gamma/psi schedules, shot budgets, and seeds are released so R2 (the SOTA collapse target) and the falsifier ladder are independently reproducible.

DOI:10.1038/s41467-018-07090-9. -- Why variational retraining of deep encodings is hard, motivating PRISM's free, non-variational estimator.

Evidence summary. The state of the art treats quantum-kernel bandwidth as one global isotropic scalar [C2, C3], which rescues generalization from exponential concentration [C1] but cannot adapt to the anisotropy of real classical data -- precisely where advantage is absent [42] and inductive bias is misaligned [C4]. PRISM promotes that scalar to a calibrated, free-to-estimate per-feature field that does double duty (inductive bias plus concentration control), acts on it with a selection gate and a continuous bandwidth, and provably collapses to the global-bandwidth SOTA at the identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6), making the proposal a strict, zero-downside generalization whose entire risk is on the upside.
